texto: I'm pretty much a dinosaur in the studio. I like things hand-drawn, even today. The story artists use Cintiqs, but I'm the only person who hasn't completely converted to computers. I like the Cintiq, but there's something about the raw emotional power of using paper and pencil.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Walt Dohrn: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a tempo. O trecho “I'm pretty much a dinosaur in the studio. I like things hand-drawn, even today. The story artists…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, today, power pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de power e story.

frase_original: I'm pretty much a dinosaur in the studio. I like things hand-drawn, even today. The story artists use Cintiqs, but I'm the only person who hasn't completely converted to computers. I like the Cintiq, but there's something about the raw emotional power of using paper and pencil.
